compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
Add a `make worktree name=<branch>` target that creates a fresh git worktree
branched from current `origin/main` and fully provisions its local env in one
step — so agents and humans stop hand-running the multi-command worktree setup
the repo's CLAUDE.md / docs/local-dev-env.md prescribe.

## Why
The repo mandates working in a fresh worktree branched from `origin/main` with its
own local venv (never the main checkout, never a stale global build). Today that is
a documented but manual sequence (`git fetch` → `git worktree add` → create venv →
`make install`), which is error-prone and easy to shortcut. A single target makes
the canonical setup one command, matches the repo's existing Makefile idiom, and
needs no new per-developer dependency (git + python + the existing `make install`).
This is the wrapper-command approach chosen over direnv: it matches "provision on
creation" semantics, works identically for humans, agents, and non-interactive CI,
and reuses the ONE canonical `make install` path rather than forking it.

## What
Add a `worktree` target to `Makefile` that:
- Requires a `name=<branch>` argument; prints usage and exits non-zero if absent.
- Creates the worktree at `../<name>` (overridable via `dir=<path>`), branched from
  freshly-fetched `origin/main` (`git fetch origin` then
  `git worktree add <dir> -b <name> origin/main`).
- Provisions the venv via the SAME sequence the docs bless and this change dogfoods:
  `python3 -m venv .venv` (seeds pip so the canonical installer works) then, with the
  venv active, `$(MAKE) install` (editable `.[dev]` install + `make hooks` wiring the
  pre-commit gate). No bespoke pip line — it delegates to the existing canonical path.
- Prints a final "activate with: cd <dir> && source .venv/bin/activate" hint.
- Is added to `.PHONY` and carries a `## ` help string so `make help` lists it.

The Gerrit `commit-msg` hook is inherited automatically (worktrees share the common
git dir's hooks), so the target does not re-fetch it — a note in the recipe records
why. Register the new target's purpose with a one-line mention in
`docs/local-dev-env.md` so it is discoverable next to the manual sequence it replaces.

## Scope
- `Makefile`: new `worktree` target + `.PHONY` entry.
- `docs/local-dev-env.md`: one line pointing at `make worktree name=<branch>` as the
  one-command form of the documented manual setup.
- Non-goal: direnv / auto-activation on `cd` (a different concern — ongoing activation,
  not creation-time provisioning); non-goal: a `post-checkout` hook; non-goal: removing
  the manual instructions (kept as the explanation of what the target automates).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `make worktree` with NO `name=` prints a usage message and exits non-zero.
      Proving command: `make worktree 2>&1; test $? -ne 0 && echo USAGE-OK`
- [ ] `make worktree name=<tmp>` creates a worktree at `../<tmp>` on a new branch
      `<tmp>` whose HEAD equals current `origin/main`, containing a populated `.venv`
      with a working `rebar` on its path.
      Proving command (run against a throwaway name, then clean up):
      `make worktree name=__wt_probe && test -x ../__wt_probe/.venv/bin/rebar && ( cd ../__wt_probe && git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD | grep -qx __wt_probe ) && echo CREATE-OK`
      Cleanup: `git worktree remove --force ../__wt_probe && git branch -D __wt_probe`
- [ ] The target is listed by `make help` (has a `## ` description) and is in `.PHONY`.
      Proving command: `make help | grep -q 'worktree' && grep -q '^\.PHONY:.*worktree' Makefile && echo HELP-OK`
- [ ] `docs/local-dev-env.md` references `make worktree name=` as the one-command setup.
      Proving command: `grep -q 'make worktree' docs/local-dev-env.md && echo DOC-OK`
- [ ] Existing gates stay green (Makefile is not Python, but the repo's checks must pass).
      Proving command: `make lint && make typecheck` both exit 0.

file_impact.reason: Add the worktree target and .PHONY entry
Reference make worktree as the one-command setup

compiled_state.title: Add 'make worktree name=<branch>' one-command worktree+venv bootstrap target
